title: ConvertKit vs Buttondown (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit and Buttondown email marketing tools. Pricing, features, and ratings to help you choose the right platform.
h1: ConvertKit vs Buttondown: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: ConvertKit and Buttondown are both popular freemium email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. ConvertKit targets creators and course sellers with advanced automation and commerce features, while Buttondown appeals to developers and technical writers who prefer markdown-based writing. Understanding their key differences will help you choose the right tool for your specific needs.
choose_a_if: You're a creator, course seller, or online business owner needing commerce and advanced automation features
You want a generous free tier supporting up to 1,000 subscribers
You're interested in earning recurring affiliate commissions by promoting the platform
choose_b_if: You prefer writing newsletters in markdown and want a developer-friendly platform
Budget is a priority and you need the lowest entry price at $9/month
You value highly-rated customer support and community feedback (4.8 G2, 4.7 Trustpilot)

faq.a: It depends on your needs. ConvertKit is better for creators with advanced requirements like commerce, visual automations, and landing pages. Buttondown is better if you're a developer or technical writer who values simplicity, markdown support, and lower pricing. Both are excellent platforms with different strengths.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or Buttondown?
faq.a: Buttondown is significantly cheaper, starting at $9/month compared to ConvertKit's $29/month. Buttondown also offers a more generous free tier with 100 subscribers versus ConvertKit's 1,000, though ConvertKit's higher limit may offset the cost difference for growing newsletters.

faq.q: Can I switch from Buttondown to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support import and export functionality, making migration possible. Buttondown explicitly lists import/export as a key feature. We recommend exporting your subscriber list and content from Buttondown, then importing it into ConvertKit. Always back up your data before switching platforms.
